--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.re-thinkingthefuture.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/A10384-Community-Resilience-and-Earthquake-Proof-Design-Image-1-1024x823.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The illustration displays high-rise buildings designed with various earthquake-resistant features, including a rocking core-wall, cross-bracing, hydraulic gyro-spheres, symmetrical construction, shape memory alloys, and shock absorbers.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.technostructacademy.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/earthquake-resistant-building-1.webp
DESCRIPTION: The design features illustrations of earthquake-resistant buildings, highlighting three key technologies: seismic resistance, vibration control, and base isolation, accompanied by the title "Earthquake Resistant Buildings: Engineering Resilience for Safety and Sustainability."

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://yojnaias.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/1-2-2048x1536.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A cityscape with sustainable infrastructure features, including solar panels, wind turbines, and green buildings, emphasizing earthquake-resilient design and renewable energy sources.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/29258.jpeg
DESCRIPTION: The chart displays the vulnerability and resilience ratings of various earthquake-prone countries based on factors like social inequality, political stability, healthcare, and overall progress, with Japan and the U.S. rated as low risk, while countries like Syria and the Philippines exhibit very high vulnerability levels.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://www.civil.utah.edu/wp-content/uploads/2023/10/Enhancing-Earthquake-Infrastructure-Resilience.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The map displays varying levels of horizontal displacement caused by earthquakes across a region, highlighting areas with different degrees of infrastructure vulnerability and resilience efforts.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Seismic activities vary globally, with regions experiencing frequent earthquakes, underscoring the need for updated design codes to enhance infrastructure resilience.

Severe earthquakes can cause catastrophic damage to buildings and infrastructure, resulting in significant economic loss and endangering lives, emphasizing the importance of effective mitigation strategies.
Developing resilient structures through advanced engineering practices is crucial to withstand seismic events, minimizing risk and ensuring safety for communities in earthquake-prone areas.
